notes: Page is React JS-rendered — requests returns empty shell. Use Algolia API directly. Credentials passed as URL query params (not headers). Paginate by batch filter W05-S26 to bypass 1000-hit cap. Deduplicate by objectID. founded_year extracted via regex from long_description.

Typesense API with header auth. Filter: is_accelerator_company=true. Page-based pagination, 250 per page max. Stop when total collected >= found. Extra fields (crunchbase_url, linkedin_url, twitter_url, worldregion) stored in extra JSON column.

Vue.js app embedded in WordPress. No clean API found in Network tab. Company data embedded as JS variable in initial HTML payload. Requires headless browser (Playwright) to extract. Deprioritized — low ROI vs effort.

WordPress server-rendered page. Company names listed as plain text under year headings. No descriptions or websites on listing page. Claude Haiku extracts names + batch_year from cleaned HTML. Set max_tokens=8192 — 4096 truncates mid-array for 250 companies. Cost ~$0.01/run.

Portfolio at /companies/ (not /portfolio — 404). Large (~441KB) server-rendered WordPress page loading all 550+ companies in a single HTML payload. No pagination needed. Each company is in div.company__item with CSS sector classes (ai, climate, fintech, etc.) for client-side filtering. Fields: name in span.company__item__name, year in h6.company__item__year, description in div.company__item__description__content, website in a.company__item__link[href]. Sector classes on the div can supplement AI detection.

startx.com/companies is an Angular SPA — scout correctly flagged it. Actual portfolio lives at web.startx.com/community, a separate Webflow site. Finsweet CMS List pagination via hashed query param 6a151520_page. Increment until no div.comn-list-item found. Fields in fs-list-field attributes. Industry may appear multiple times per company.

WordPress admin-ajax.php endpoint. POST with action=filter and query JSON in body. Paginate by incrementing paged in query JSON. Use max_page from first response. Parse tile--company divs from content HTML. founded_year available in meta__row structure. Website not in listing view.

Algolia meta tag present but DevTools confirms no XHR calls fire — server handles Algolia queries and renders results directly. Only 100 of 814 companies in initial HTML payload; remaining load via path-based pagination at /ventures/p2, /ventures/p3 etc. (NOT ?page=2). Each page has 100 companies; stop when fewer than 100 returned. 9 pages total. Each card is a.venture-card with lab affiliation as a second CSS class (student-i-lab, launch-lab, pagliuca-harvard-life-lab, no-lab-affiliation). No website or founding year in listing view.

URL redirects to sbs.ox.ac.uk (Oxford Saïd Business School). Only 4 cohorts (2018-2021) with companies on separate subpages — program appears wound down. ~40-50 companies total across all cohorts. Not worth engineering effort. Lesson: verify program is still active before building a scraper.

Static GET pagination via ?0b933bfd_page=N. The hash key is Webflow-generated and could change — detect it from the a.w-pagination-next link on each page rather than hardcoding. ~52 cards per page. Card container: div.portco_card. Fields use fs-cmsfilter-field attributes: name (p), description (p), sector/location/year via div.tag_small_wrap distinguished by their own fs-cmsfilter-field value ('sector', 'year', or the location string itself). Website from a.clickable_link[href]. Previous assessment that Antler required Playwright was based on cmsload/cmsfilter; the portfolio listing actually uses cmslist with interceptable GET pagination.

Drupal CMS, server-rendered. Listing at /people/teams-startups-filtered?program-filter[18]=18, paginated &page=0 through &page=8 (9 pages total). Each card has name, short description, program track (eLab Accelerator / eLab Incubator), cohort year, and slug. Detail pages at /people/startups-teams/{slug} have full description and team members. No external website URLs available anywhere on the site.

WordPress site using admin-ajax.php with action=company_filtration_query. Returns all 800+ companies in a single JSON response — no pagination. Fields returned: title, url, class (batch number). No description available from this endpoint. POST params use duplicate keys (meta[0][], meta[1][], meta[2][]) so must be sent as a list of tuples not a dict. X-Requested-With: XMLHttpRequest header required.

REST API at /api/organizations?role=company&page_idx=N&sort=latest_update. No auth required. Paginate via page_idx (1-indexed); total pages in meta.page_count. Returns ~6,200 companies across ~311 pages. Fields include name, homepage_url, founded_on, total_funding_usd, last_funding_event (series, money_raised_usd, announced_on). No descriptions available from this endpoint. Site is a fully JS-rendered SPA returning a 940-byte HTML shell on all routes — no content visible via static fetch. API discovered via DevTools Network tab by loading the page in Chrome and inspecting XHR requests. Pattern: SPA sites with empty HTML shells often have a clean REST API backend; check Network tab for /api/ calls when the page loads. Auth: none required for this site — session cookies present in browser requests but not enforced server-side.

Single page, all companies in static HTML. Companies grouped by class header (e.g. 'Class 13 | May 15 - August 1, 2025') inside accordion panels (div.accordion-panel). Name is in <strong> tag; description is the full li text. Cohort year extracted from the date range in the header. Older classes (pre-2023) have only names (links or plain text) with no descriptions. Some cohort headers list two class numbers (e.g. 'Class 11, Class 2') — both stored as-is in batch field.

JS-rendered portfolio with a Load More button. Only ~30 of 450+ companies are visible in the initial HTML payload. No Algolia, Typesense, WordPress REST API, or __NEXT_DATA__ JSON blob detected. All pagination and filtering is client-side with no observable API endpoint. Would require Playwright headless browser to scrape fully. Skipped as low ROI given the engineering overhead.

When ?page=2 returns identical content to page 1, do not conclude pagination is API-only. Also try path-based variants: /p2, /page/2, /page-2. Query params (?page=2, ?p=2, ?offset=100) and path-based (/p2, /page/2, /page-2) are both common. Path-based is typical on WordPress, Drupal, and custom university CMS builds. Example: Harvard Innovation Labs uses /ventures/p2 — ?page=2 returned identical results and was incorrectly flagged as no-effect. Always try both before escalating to API investigation.

Angular SPA — requests returns empty HTML shell. Portfolio at /companies/ has no visible company data in static HTML. No Algolia, Typesense, __NEXT_DATA__, or observable REST API endpoint detected via static HTTP inspection. Requires DevTools network tab investigation to find the data layer. Excluded pending further investigation.

Next.js client-rendered — requests returns empty HTML shell. Portfolio at /companies has no visible company data in static HTML. No __NEXT_DATA__ JSON blob or observable REST API endpoint detected via static HTTP inspection. Requires DevTools network tab investigation to find the data layer. Excluded pending further investigation.

Always use word-boundary regex (\b + keyword + \b) for short AI keywords — bare substring search matched 'ml' in 'marketplace' and caused 81.7% false positives. Critically: always include 'ai' explicitly in the keyword list — it was missing from the global list and caused systematic undercounting across all sources (Crunchbase +15.9k rows, YC +760 when fixed). Run a spot-check of AI% after adding each new source to catch missing keywords early.

Algolia hard-caps results at 1,000 hits per query regardless of total. To retrieve full datasets, loop through a filterable field (e.g. batch names for YC: W05, S05, W06...) and run one query per value, then deduplicate by objectID. This retrieved 5,365 YC companies vs 1,000 from a single paginated query. Identify filterable fields from the Algolia facets response or from UI filter controls on the page.

Always set max_tokens=8192 (Haiku's max) when using Claude for bulk HTML extraction. Without this, Claude truncates its response mid-JSON — the partial output parses successfully but silently drops companies from the tail. This is not an obvious error: no exception is thrown, counts just come in low. Set max_tokens explicitly on every bulk extraction call.

Anthropic API returns HTTP 529 (Overloaded) as a transient error under load. Always wrap Anthropic API calls in retry logic: exponential backoff starting at 10s, up to 3 retries. Do not treat 529 as a permanent failure — it resolves on retry. Log the attempt count so operators can see if the service was under sustained load during a run.

A UNIQUE(name, source) constraint silently drops duplicate companies when inserting across multiple batches of the same source — this is expected and correct behavior. Document it in run summaries so operators don't mistake the lower insert count for a bug. If preserving all batch memberships matters (e.g. a company appeared in YC W21 and YC S23), use a junction table instead of a flat unique constraint.
